reason: The missing row-correspondence rule is a structural constraint that directly governs how developers must lay out multi-entity test data in Excel. Without it, a developer has no basis to know that row N of sysAcctEntity, row N of usersEntity, and row N of grpSysAcctEntity must all correspond to the same test case. Incorrect data alignment would silently produce wrong test inputs, leading to either false-passing or misleading-failing tests with no obvious diagnostic trail.

findings_assessment.location: s5 — テストデータ(入力値)の作成
findings_assessment.justification: The row-correspondence rule ('各データの同じ行で1組のテストデータとなる') is the only rule that tells developers how to align rows across multiple entity sheets into a single test case. Without it, a developer writing a test for a method that takes multiple entity arguments (e.g. registerUser(sysAcct, users, grpSysAcct)) has no guidance on Excel data structure. They could easily place case-2 data for usersEntity on row 1 while case-1 data for sysAcctEntity is also on row 1, producing silently incorrect test inputs. This is not recoverable from the code samples alone because the loop index `i` is used uniformly across all three `getListMap` calls — the row-alignment contract is implied but never stated. Phase E did not resolve this: the s5 content in the Phase E output is identical to Phase D (the output is truncated mid-sentence, indicating the fix attempt produced incomplete output and the change was never applied).
